contract.trigger: When a core workflow needs to be automated across various platforms.

input_file.description: The input file containing data or configurations for the workflow.

output_file.description: The output file generated after processing the input.

preconditions: The input file must be in a supported format.
The necessary permissions to access the input file must be granted.
postconditions: The output file is generated successfully.
The workflow is logged for auditing purposes.
contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates a common workflow pattern observed across multiple parent skills, allowing for consistent automation processes.

contract.trigger: When setting up a new automation task using Rube MCP.

toolkit.description: The specific toolkit to connect with Rube MCP.

connection_status.description: The status of the connection to the specified toolkit.

preconditions: Rube MCP must be connected (RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS available)
Active connection via RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with the specified toolkit
postconditions: Connection status shows ACTIVE before running any workflows
contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common setup procedure for various automations using Rube MCP.

contract.trigger: When a workflow requires tool execution based on specific use cases.

use_case.description: The specific use case for which tools are being discovered.

session_id.description: The session ID for the current workflow.

tools.description: A list of available tools with their slugs and input schemas.

preconditions: Rube MCP must be connected.
Active connection via RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with the appropriate toolkit.
postconditions: Tools are discovered and ready for execution.
Session ID is reused for subsequent operations.
contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure of discovering and executing tools across various parent skills.

contract.trigger: When automating operations through Composio's various toolkits via Rube MCP.

toolkit_name.description: The name of the toolkit to be used for automation.

result.description: The result of the automation operation.

The result of the operation is returned.

contract.trigger: When a connection to a service needs to be established or verified.

session_id.description: The session identifier for the connection.

contract.trigger: When a specific task needs to be automated using available tools.

use_case.description: The specific task to be automated.

session.description: The session ID for the current automation context.

available_tools.description: A list of tools available for the specified task.

contract.trigger: When a Rube MCP connection is required for automation tasks.

contract.trigger: When the agent needs to find a specific object in the environment.

target_object.description: The name of the object to locate.

suspected_receptacle.description: The receptacle where the object is likely to be found.

object_location.description: The location of the target object if found, or a note of its absence.

preconditions: The agent has identified the target object and its suspected location.
The suspected receptacle is accessible.
postconditions: The agent has either located the target object or confirmed its absence.
The agent may need to take further actions based on the object's status.
contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure of locating a target object within a suspected receptacle, observed across multiple parent skills.

contract.trigger: Use this skill when the agent needs to interact with a closed receptacle to access its contents or prepare it for object placement.

action_result.description: The result of the action taken on the receptacle, indicating success or failure.

preconditions: The receptacle is closed and needs to be opened for interaction.
The agent has finished inspecting an open receptacle and is ready to close it.
postconditions: The receptacle is opened, allowing access to its contents.
The receptacle is closed, maintaining a tidy environment.
contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedures for opening and closing receptacles across multiple parent skills.

contract.trigger: When a connection to a specific toolkit needs to be established or verified.

toolkit.description: The name of the toolkit to connect to.

session_id.description: The session ID for the current connection.

postconditions: Connection status is confirmed as ACTIVE or INACTIVE

contract.trigger: When the agent is holding an object that needs to be heated, cooled, or cleaned using an appliance.

object.description: The name of the object to be processed (e.g., 'potato 1', 'egg 1').

appliance.description: The name of the appliance to be used (e.g., 'microwave 1', 'fridge 1').

action.description: The action to perform on the object, such as 'heat', 'cool', or 'clean'.

result.description: The state of the object after processing (e.g., 'The egg 1 is heated.').

preconditions: The agent is holding the object.
The agent is at the location of the specified appliance.
postconditions: The object's state has been modified according to the action performed.

required_tool.description: The appliance or instrument needed (e.g., sinkbasin, microwave).

interaction_type.description: The action verb to apply (e.g., clean, heat, cool, use).

result.description: Confirmation of the action performed on the target object.

preconditions: Both the tool and target object are in the agent's possession or within reach.
The agent knows which interaction verb to use for the task context.
postconditions: The target object has been acted upon using the specified tool.
The task is complete.
contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure of locating a tool, applying it to a target object, and confirming the action across multiple parent skills.
